title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in shared-source and tenant subscription model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Source Registration
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 8886 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.

Inspect the Feed Go Source Registration trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Source Registration.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Concurrent Registration
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-concurrent-registration-e6pcx9" that uses case 9620 to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
